summary: Dependency-confusion beacon exfiltrates installer hostname to attacker-controlled OAST endpoint on require()
details: On require/import, index.js reads the installer's hostname via os.hostname() and issues an HTTP GET to a Burp Collaborator subdomain at api-client.<hostname>.200majoeu01dk02xnjdajro1isojc90y.oastify.com (index.js:4-7). The package is published under the @onum-releases scope by an unrelated author and self-describes in package.json as a dependency-confusion / scope-takeover demonstration targeting Onum's namespace. Any internal build that resolves this squatted scope from the public registry will leak its hostname to the attacker's collaborator listener, fingerprinting the victim environment for follow-on attacks. The behavior fires automatically on module load — no user action required beyond install + require.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
